message:
Close three bd-9chy.* stdio epic subtasks and open bd-r2oq search.h task

Reconcile the frankenlibc beads ledger with the actual stdio-epic
progress that other sessions already landed, and record a new
search.h conformance-coverage task as in_progress so triage tools see
it on the next pass.

Status transitions in this commit:

- bd-9chy.16 "stdio: implement adopt_foreign_file via membrane bloom
  precheck + fd extraction" — in_progress -> closed (close_reason
  "done", closed_at 2026-04-15T21:01:08Z). The adoption path, the
  per-entrypoint bloom precheck, the evidence ring buffer hook, and
  the generation-safety stale-pointer EBADF path are all in tree.
- bd-9chy.17 "stdio: per-FILE parking_lot::ReentrantMutex +
  flockfile/funlockfile/ftrylockfile + *_unlocked" — in_progress ->
  closed (closed_at 2026-04-15T20:20:27Z). The reentrant mutex, the
  explicit lock/unlock entrypoints, the *_unlocked variants with the
  debug-assertion lock-held check, and the 32-thread stress test are
  all landed.
- bd-9chy.2 "support_matrix: tighten classifier to inspect ABI
  function bodies and add WrapsHostLibc state" — in_progress ->
  closed (closed_at 2026-04-16T09:51:58Z, close_reason "Completed
  body-aware WrapsHostLibc classifier, maintenance gate, and
  closure-contract integration"). The new taxonomy state, the body
  classifier, the maintenance gate, and the closure-contract clause
  are all in place.
- bd-9chy.46 "test: NativeFile layout — comprehensive offset
  assertion + size + alignment + glibc-version-matrix unit suite" —
  in_progress -> closed (closed_at 2026-04-15T20:20:14Z). The
  memoffset assertion battery, the size/align invariants, the vtable
  offset, the generation/fingerprint coverage, and the fuzz harness
  all exist.

New issue added:

- bd-r2oq "Add search.h conformance fixtures and harness coverage" —
  status: in_progress, priority 2 (P2 / medium), type: task. Scope:
  a dedicated `tests/conformance/fixtures/search_ops.json` fixture
  plus `frankenlibc-harness` coverage for the <search.h> ABI surface
  (hcreate/hsearch/hdestroy, tsearch/tfind/tdelete/twalk, lfind,
  lsearch, insque, remque), following the existing conformance
  fixture patterns and validating schema/spec coverage.

No source, fixtures, or tests change here — the individual work was
already landed; this commit just catches the JSONL ledger up so the
audit trail stops lying about what is still open. `.beads/issues.jsonl`
is force-added because `.beads/` is gitignored while the JSONL itself
is tracked.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-9chy.10","title":"stdio: export _IO_2_1_stdin_/stdout_/stderr_ aliases bound to NativeFile storage","description":"## Background

Plan v4 §3 (final paragraph). Some statically-linked-against-libc binaries resolve _IO_2_1_stdin_, _IO_2_1_stdout_, _IO_2_1_stderr_ directly via dlsym or link-time resolution, bypassing the public stdin/stdout/stderr aliases. Without exporting these names from FrankenLibC, the binary sees two distinct streams (one native FrankenLibC, one host glibc) and the program misbehaves subtly: partial flushes, lost output, double-close.

## Depends on

bd-9chy.1 (NativeFile layout)

## What to do

1. Add the three symbol names to crates/frankenlibc-abi/version_scripts/libc.map under the GLIBC_2.2.5 version tag.
2. In stdio_abi.rs (or io_internal_abi.rs), declare the underscored names as #[no_mangle] static aliases pointing to the *same* NativeFile storage as the public stdin/stdout/stderr globals. Do NOT allocate a second set of NativeFile objects — they must be the same object so a flush via stdin reaches the same buffer as a flush via _IO_2_1_stdin_.
3. Verify the alias by building the .so and inspecting nm: nm -D target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so | grep _IO_2_1 must show the three symbols at the same address as stdin/stdout/stderr.
4. Add an integration test: build a small C program that takes both stdin and _IO_2_1_stdin_, asserts they are equal, and runs it under LD_PRELOAD.

## Done

- libc.map exports the three aliases
- nm verification passes
- Integration test passes
- The aliases survive a release build with strip+lto","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:28:41.061649453Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-13T19:10:43.305878505Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation","stdio","version-script"]}
 {"id":"bd-9chy.15","title":"stdio: build NATIVE_IO_JUMP_T vtable + 17 trampolines for indirect dispatch","description":"## Background

Plan v4 §10. Many glibc-built binaries (especially with -fno-plt) call vfprintf indirectly through ((struct _IO_FILE_plus *)fp)->vtable->__xsputn(...) rather than through the PLT-resolved vfprintf symbol. If our vtable pointer is null or wrong, the program crashes inside __xsputn.

## Depends on

bd-9chy.1 (NativeFile must already have a vtable slot)

## What to do

1. In crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/io_internal_abi.rs, define repr(C) struct _IO_jump_t with the 17 standard slots: __dummy, __dummy2, __finish, __overflow, __underflow, __uflow, __pbackfail, __xsputn, __xsgetn, __seekoff, __seekpos, __setbuf, __sync, __doallocate, __read, __write, __seek, __close, __stat, __showmanyc, __imbue. Field offsets pinned to glibc 2.34.
2. Create a static NATIVE_IO_JUMP_T: _IO_jump_t = ... initialized with extern "C" function pointers to Rust trampolines.
3. Each trampoline takes a *mut FILE, casts to *mut NativeFile, acquires the recursive lock, and dispatches to the native kernel function used by the public ABI entrypoint. The trampolines for __xsputn and __xsgetn are the high-volume ones; benchmark them.
4. Every NativeFile constructor (bd-9chy.5..10) sets `self.vtable = &NATIVE_IO_JUMP_T as *const _ as *mut _`.
5. Trampoline must handle the foreign-pointer-adoption case: if the FILE* it receives has the bloom precheck saying "not ours," route through adopt_foreign_file (separate bead bd-9chy.16). For now, panic with a clear message; the next bead wires real adoption.
6. Unit test: build a small Rust test that takes &mut NATIVE_IO_JUMP_T.__xsputn, calls it with a NativeFile* and verifies bytes are written.

## Done

- _IO_jump_t struct defined with offsets matching glibc
- NATIVE_IO_JUMP_T static built and wired into NativeFile constructors
- All 17 trampolines have implementations (some can be no-ops or default behavior)
- Unit test for indirect __xsputn dispatch passes
- Snapshot test tests/runtime_math/golden/io_jump_dispatch.v1.json captures vtable-vs-PLT call counts on a representative workload","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:30:03.906540102Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-13T17:38:04.026649614Z","closed_at":"2026-04-13T17:38:04.026388970Z","close_reason":"Implemented _IO_jump_t vtable with 21 trampolines for indirect dispatch. All slots wired to native stdio functions. NativeFile::new() sets vtable to NATIVE_IO_JUMP_T. Layout tests added. Also fixed TLS corruption and type mismatches.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["stdio","vtable"]}
-{"id":"bd-9chy.16","title":"stdio: implement adopt_foreign_file via membrane bloom precheck + fd extraction","description":"## Background

Plan v4 §11. When a dlopend library opens a file BEFORE LD_PRELOAD takes effect and then hands the FILE* back to the LD_PRELOAD-ed binary, the pointer is host glibcs, not ours. Today the code "handles" this by delegating to host stdio (which is exactly the problem the epic exists to fix). Option B requires we *adopt* such pointers into the native registry instead.

## Depends on

bd-9chy.15 (vtable trampolines need a place to call the adoption path)

## What to do

1. In crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdio_abi.rs (or io_internal_abi.rs), add `pub(crate) fn adopt_foreign_file(fp: *mut FILE) -> *mut NativeFile`.
2. Step 1 of every public stdio entrypoint AND every vtable trampoline: run the membrane bloom-filter ownership precheck (existing primitive in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/bloom.rs). Cost: ~10 ns. Well within strict-mode budget.
3. If precheck says "ours," dispatch to the kernel directly.
4. If precheck says "not ours," call adopt_foreign_file:
   a. Read ((_IO_FILE *)fp)->_fileno to recover the underlying fd. (We do NOT read other fields — they may be in a different layout from glibcs.) The _fileno offset is fixed at offset 0x70 in glibc 2.34 for x86_64; pin this constant in code with a comment explaining why we deliberately read it raw.
   b. Allocate a NativeFile shell, set its fd to the recovered value, set buffering mode based on isatty(fileno) (raw syscall), set orientation = Undecided, vtable = &NATIVE_IO_JUMP_T.
   c. Insert into stream registry indexed by BOTH the original foreign pointer AND the new native pointer (so subsequent calls with the foreign pointer find the same NativeFile).
   d. Emit a FOREIGN_FILE_ADOPTED evidence record (uses the schema from bd-9chy.4).
   e. Return the new NativeFile *.
5. Generation safety: if the same fd is later closed and reopened, the new NativeFile gets a fresh generation; the old foreign-pointer registry entry is invalidated. Subsequent stdio calls against the stale foreign pointer return EBADF and emit FOREIGN_FILE_STALE.
6. Test: build a test C program that calls dlopen("libc.so.6") and uses dlsym to get fopen, opens a file via that dlsym-resolved fopen, and passes the FILE* to FrankenLibCs fread. Verify the adoption happens (assert via the evidence ring buffer).

## Done

- adopt_foreign_file implemented
- Every stdio entrypoint and trampoline runs the bloom precheck first
- Adoption test passes
- Evidence records appear in the ring buffer for the test
- Stale-pointer EBADF path covered by a separate unit test","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:30:24.854354196Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-13T19:18:17.183497203Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foreign-adoption","membrane","stdio"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.16","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:30:24.854354196Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-9chy.17","title":"stdio: per-FILE parking_lot::ReentrantMutex + flockfile/funlockfile/ftrylockfile + *_unlocked","description":"## Background

Plan v4 §1.11. POSIX requires every standard stdio function to be thread-safe via implicit per-FILE locking, with flockfile/funlockfile/ftrylockfile for explicit batching, and the *_unlocked family for callers that already hold the lock. v1 ignored this entirely.

## Depends on

bd-9chy.1 (NativeFile must already have the lock slot)

## What to do

1. NativeFileState contains parking_lot::ReentrantMutex<NativeFileLocked>. The locked region wraps buffer cursors, eof/error flags, ungetc, mbstate, orientation, healing budget.
2. Every public ABI fn (fread, fwrite, ...) acquires the lock via reentrant_mutex.lock() before entering the kernel; releases on exit (RAII via guard).
3. Every *_unlocked variant (fread_unlocked, fwrite_unlocked, fgetc_unlocked, fputc_unlocked, fgets_unlocked, fputs_unlocked, feof_unlocked, ferror_unlocked, clearerr_unlocked, fileno_unlocked) skips the acquisition AND under #[cfg(debug_assertions)] asserts the caller already holds the lock (parking_lots ReentrantMutex exposes is_locked / try_lock for this).
4. flockfile / funlockfile / ftrylockfile (verify they exist in stdio_abi.rs; if not, add them) operate on the same recursive mutex.
5. The recursive lock count must survive setjmp/longjmp correctly (deferred to bd-9chy.18, the longjmp lock-stack bead).
6. Add an integration test that fans out 32 threads, each writing 1024 4-byte records to a shared FILE*; reads back; verifies record-level interleaving (no torn writes). Both strict and hardened modes.

## Done

- Every public ABI fn takes the lock; every *_unlocked fn does not
- ReentrantMutex used (not Mutex — POSIX requires recursive)
- 32-thread stress test passes in both modes
- flockfile/funlockfile/ftrylockfile work correctly under nested lock","notes":"Implemented flockfile/funlockfile/ftrylockfile. NativeFile already has ReentrantMutex in NativeFileState. Remaining: verify all ABI fns use locking, audit *_unlocked variants, add stress test.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:30:53.015024994Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-13T19:06:29.580638029Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["locking","stdio"]}
-{"id":"bd-9chy.2","title":"support_matrix: tighten classifier to inspect ABI function bodies and add WrapsHostLibc state","description":"## Background

Reality-check found that tests/conformance/support_matrix_maintenance_report.v1.json was regenerated 2026-04-08 with zero deltas vs the 2026-02-18 baseline, despite ongoing March/April commits to ABI files including stdio. The current classifier in scripts/generate_support_matrix_maintenance.py (and the Rust harness equivalent in crates/frankenlibc-harness if it exists) inspects only signatures, not bodies. As a result, ABI functions that are labelled Implemented but in fact call libc::fwrite or host_*_fn() at runtime are not flagged.

This bead is the body-inspecting classifier and the new explicit WrapsHostLibc taxonomy state. It runs *first* in the epic order because every other bead depends on having a mechanical way to verify "did I close the gap I think I closed."

## What to do

1. Add a new variant WrapsHostLibc to the support taxonomy (alongside Implemented, RawSyscall, GlibcCallThrough, Stub). Update the JSON schema, the Rust enum if any, and the README/AGENTS.md taxonomy descriptions.
2. Extend scripts/generate_support_matrix_maintenance.py to walk every *_abi.rs file in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/ via syn (or tree-sitter, or naive ripgrep — the rule is structural).
3. For each public extern "C" fn, check the body for: any libc::f* call (fopen/fclose/fread/fwrite/fseek/ftell/fflush/setvbuf/ungetc/feof/ferror/clearerr/fileno/getline/getdelim/printf), any libc::malloc/free/realloc, any host_*_fn() invocation, any crate::host_resolve::resolve_host_symbol_* call. If any match, classify as WrapsHostLibc.
4. Regenerate support_matrix.json. EXPECT a one-time bump: stdio symbols + rpc/glibc_internal symbols flip from Implemented to WrapsHostLibc. This is the corrective action — the count change is the audit telling the truth for the first time.
5. Add the L0 closure gate clause in tests/conformance/closure_contract.v1.json: WrapsHostLibc must be 0 for L0 closure.
6. Run tests/conformance/check_support_matrix_maintenance.sh and confirm it now produces a non-trivial delta vs the prior baseline.

## Done

- New WrapsHostLibc state exists in schema and code
- Classifier inspects function bodies, not signatures
- support_matrix.json regenerates with the corrective bump
- closure_contract.v1.json gates on WrapsHostLibc == 0
- CI fails on a freshly added libc::fwrite in any *_abi.rs file (verify with a one-line test PR)
- README and AGENTS.md taxonomy descriptions updated to mention WrapsHostLibc","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:26:27.159467007Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T18:02:46.438166117Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["classifier","foundation","stdio"]}

 {"id":"bd-9chy.26","title":"stdio: build offline synthesis pipeline tools/stdio_synth/ for printf/scanf grammars + table compiler + SMT prover","description":"## Background

Plan v4 §1.4 + §1.8 + §16. Stdio is reverse-round anchor #5 in AGENTS.md ("eliminate parser-state explosions and locale drift; ship generated parser/transducer tables"). The Required Methodology says: spec → synthesis → compact runtime artifact + proof. The current hand-written crates/frankenlibc-core/src/stdio/printf.rs (1446 LOC) and scanf.rs (1026 LOC) are NOT what the methodology calls for.

This bead replaces them with a build-time synthesis pipeline that emits proven tables.

## What to do

1. Create tools/stdio_synth/ (NEW directory, lives OUTSIDE the libc workspace — does not pollute the .so):
   - spec/printf_grammar.json: formal grammar of POSIX printf format language as a typed regular tree (flag set × width × precision × length × conversion).
   - spec/scanf_grammar.json: same for scanf.
   - synth/printf_compiler.rs: Rust binary; consumes the grammar; emits a const dispatch table PRINTF_TABLE: [PrintfRoute; 256] into crates/frankenlibc-core/src/stdio/printf_tables.rs.
   - synth/proof.rs: for each generated route, emits an SMT-LIB script asserting that the routes behavior matches a small reference interpreter encoded in the grammar. Run via cvc5 (or z3) at build time. Build fails on any unproven obligation.
   - synth/coverage.json: generated covering-array confirming every (flag × length × conversion) interaction is exercised. AGENTS.md obligation #17 (covering-array conformance interaction coverage).
   - synth/symmetry.rs: define the symmetry group acting on the (route × invariant) lattice (AGENTS.md obligation #35 Atiyah-Bott localization). Reuses the existing crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/atiyah_bott.rs primitive.
   - synth/compress.rs: consumes the symmetry group and the obligation set, emits a quotiented DAG. SMT obligations discharged on the quotient (~120 obligations instead of ~30k = 250x reduction).
2. Hand-written crates/frankenlibc-core/src/stdio/printf.rs becomes a 200-line dispatcher consuming the generated table, plus float-printing helpers (Grisu/Ryu — not table-friendly).
3. Same architecture for scanf.
4. Build-time budget: printf+scanf SMT phase ≤ 90 s on a single core. Gated by tools/stdio_synth/check_smt_budget.sh.
5. Wire into Cargo.toml build.rs.

## Why this is its own bead and why it is high-priority

Without this, the project is "porting glibc by hand," which AGENTS.md "Clean-Room Porting" rule #2 explicitly forbids. With it, the Atiyah-Bott controller moves from "telemetry-only" to "actively used at build time," which is a small-but-real win for the runtime_math honesty story.

## Done

- tools/stdio_synth/ exists with grammar, compiler, prover, symmetry, compress, coverage
- printf.rs and scanf.rs reduced to <300 LOC dispatchers + table consumers
- printf_tables.rs and scanf_tables.rs are auto-generated and checked-in
- cargo build invokes the synthesis and the prover; build fails on any unproven obligation
- check_smt_budget.sh keeps SMT cost ≤ 90 s
- The generated tables byte-equal across runs (deterministic)","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:33:20.407269611Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-14T05:03:28.764590026Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["methodology","stdio","synthesis"]}
 {"id":"bd-9chy.3","title":"stdio: encode 11 invariants in tests/conformance/stdio_invariants.v1.json + harness verifier","description":"## Background

Plan v4 §1.5 enumerates the 11 per-entrypoint invariants for stdio (fopen, fread, fwrite, fseek, ftell, fclose, ungetc, fflush, fileno, feof/ferror, clearerr) as proof obligations on the native kernel. Currently they live in prose inside the plan doc; this bead encodes them as a machine-checkable contract.

## What to do

1. Create tests/conformance/stdio_invariants.v1.json with one entry per entrypoint. Each entry has: function name, pre-conditions (boolean expressions over input parameters and the registry state), post-conditions (boolean expressions over return value, errno, the registry, and observable side effects), and the lattice transition (which SafetyState the call may move the FILE through).
2. Define a tiny invariant DSL that can express "fp is in registry," "fp.fd >= 0," "return <= n," "fp.eof set if return < n," etc. JSON form is fine; no need for a real parser.
3. Extend crates/frankenlibc-harness/src/verify.rs (or add a new module verify_invariants.rs) to load the invariants file and check each invariant against every fixture run from tests/conformance/fixtures/stdio/. The verifier emits a structured pass/fail per (fixture, invariant) cell.
4. Wire into scripts/check_conformance_fixture_pipeline.sh — failures block the gate.
5. The 11 invariants are listed verbatim in plan v4 §1.5 and must be encoded faithfully. Phase 5 refinement may add more (e.g. for popen, tmpfile, fmemopen, fopencookie, getline) once those entrypoints have native implementations.

## Why this matters

Without machine-checkable invariants, "fread post-condition" lives in someones head. With this bead, every fixture run is a witness that the invariants held. The lattice transition column also feeds into the runtime_math cohomology controller (which monitors cross-shard consistency on stdio state), tightening §12 of the plan.

## Done

- stdio_invariants.v1.json exists with 11 entries
- Verifier loads and checks them
- Existing fixture runs report no invariant violations
- A deliberately-broken test fixture (e.g. an fread that returns more than n) is correctly flagged","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:26:42.856714759Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-13T23:25:59.360150007Z","closed_at":"2026-04-13T23:25:59.359488084Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation","invariants","stdio"]}
 {"id":"bd-9chy.4","title":"stdio: define versioned JSONL evidence schema + parser + ring buffer","description":"## Background

Plan v4 §14. Every healing event, every foreign-pointer adoption, every POMDP repair decision, every membrane stage attribution emits a JSONL row to target/conformance/stdio_evidence.jsonl. The schema is versioned and a parser lives in crates/frankenlibc-harness/src/stdio_evidence.rs.

## What to do

Define the schema (schema_version=1) per plan v4 §14: timestamp_unix_ns, process (pid/tid/comm), mode, event_kind enum, function name, fp_hex, fp_origin enum, fd, params object, result object (return/errno/elapsed_ns), membrane_stages object (per-stage ns), runtime_math object (pomdp_action / conformal_band / cohomology_consistent / cvar_alarm), healing_action option, evidence_ring_seq monotonic counter, trace_id uuid.
2. Add a thread-local trace_id generator in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/evidence.rs (which already exists per the audit at runtime_math/evidence.rs — extend rather than create).
3. Write the JSONL serializer (use serde_json with a stable field order). Atomic ring buffer for backpressure: drop oldest on overflow with a counter increment so consumers see the drop.

 {"id":"bd-9chy.41","title":"ci: glibc version matrix (2.31, 2.34, 2.38) for NativeFile field-offset assertions","description":"## Background

Plan v4 §3. NativeFile is layout-compatible with glibcs _IO_FILE for the first 216 bytes. The pinned offsets are taken from glibc 2.34 (the dominant Ubuntu LTS version). But binaries built against earlier or later glibc may have slightly different offsets if upstream silently rearranged. CI must verify the offsets are correct on the relevant distro versions.

## Depends on

bd-9chy.1 (NativeFile layout)

## What to do

1. Add a CI matrix entry that runs the field-offset assertion test against:
   - glibc 2.31 (Ubuntu 20.04, RHEL 8)
   - glibc 2.34 (Ubuntu 22.04, baseline)
   - glibc 2.38 (Ubuntu 24.04, RHEL 9)
2. Use a simple Docker image per row, or buildjet matrix if available.
3. The test inspects /usr/include/bits/types/struct_FILE.h on the runner, extracts the field offsets via offsetof() in a small C program, and compares against the pinned constants in NativeFile.
4. CI fails if any offset diverges. Divergence triggers a Phase 5 refinement round on bd-9chy.1.
5. Document the policy in STDIO_NATIVE_REPLACEMENT_PLAN.md §3 (already there) and reference this CI matrix.

## Done

- CI matrix has 3 glibc rows
- Each runs the offset-extraction test
- All 3 are green at v0
- Documented in plan and CI configuration","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:37:45.968711931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-14T04:32:20.998990360Z","closed_at":"2026-04-14T04:32:20.998371338Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","multi-glibc","stdio"]}
-{"id":"bd-9chy.46","title":"test: NativeFile layout — comprehensive offset assertion + size + alignment + glibc-version-matrix unit suite","description":"## Background

bd-9chy.1 implements the _IO_FILE_plus layout. This bead is the comprehensive test surface for that work. Separated because the test list is long enough to warrant its own tracking.

## Depends on

bd-9chy.1 (the layout itself)

## Test list

1. Field-offset assertions for every glibc 2.34 _IO_FILE field via memoffset::offset_of! macro pinned to numeric constants extracted from /usr/include/bits/types/struct_FILE.h. Fields: _flags, _IO_read_ptr, _IO_read_end, _IO_read_base, _IO_write_base, _IO_write_ptr, _IO_write_end, _IO_buf_base, _IO_buf_end, _IO_save_base, _IO_backup_base, _IO_save_end, _markers, _chain, _fileno, _flags2, _old_offset, _cur_column, _vtable_offset, _shortbuf, _lock, _offset, _codecvt, _wide_data, _freeres_list, _freeres_buf, _pad5, _mode, _unused2.
2. size_of::<NativeFile>() <= 4096 (slab compat).
3. align_of::<NativeFile>() >= align_of::<*mut c_void>() (vtable alignment).
4. The vtable field offset is exactly at offset 216 (end of _IO_FILE region).
5. The reentrant mutex is positioned AFTER the vtable so a foreign pointer reading the first 216 bytes does not accidentally pull mutex bytes.
6. mbstate_t is initialized to the C-locale initial state on construction.
7. Generation counter starts at a fresh non-zero value (test that two consecutive constructions produce different generations).
8. The fingerprint is a function of (fd, generation, address) — verify two NativeFiles with same fd but different generation have distinct fingerprints.
9. The orientation field default is Undecided (0).
10. Multi-glibc CI matrix (delegated to bd-9chy.41): all assertions hold on glibc 2.31, 2.34, 2.38.
11. Fuzz: cargo fuzz target that constructs NativeFiles with random parameters and checks the invariants in §1.5 of plan v4.

## Done

- All 11 test categories implemented as #[test]s in crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/native_file_layout_test.rs
- Multi-glibc CI matrix from bd-9chy.41 runs them
- Fuzz target compiles and runs for at least 60 seconds in CI without finding violations","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:40:11.282599285Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-14T04:36:26.089253023Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation","stdio","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.46","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy.41","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:18:14.533235042Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-9chy.47","title":"test: fopen/fdopen comprehensive unit + integration suite (mode parsing, isatty, errno, fd lifecycle, EINTR)","description":"## Background

bd-9chy.5 lists only one unit test in its done criteria. This bead is the comprehensive surface.

## Depends on

bd-9chy.5

## Test list

Mode parsing:
1. fopen("/tmp/x", "r") → O_RDONLY
2. fopen("/tmp/x", "w") → O_WRONLY|O_CREAT|O_TRUNC, mode 0666
3. fopen("/tmp/x", "a") → O_WRONLY|O_CREAT|O_APPEND
4. fopen("/tmp/x", "r+") → O_RDWR
5. fopen("/tmp/x", "w+") → O_RDWR|O_CREAT|O_TRUNC
6. fopen("/tmp/x", "a+") → O_RDWR|O_CREAT|O_APPEND
7. Each of the above with trailing "b" (binary, no-op on Linux)
8. Each of the above with trailing "x" (O_EXCL — fail if exists)
9. Trailing "e" (O_CLOEXEC) honored
10. Bad mode strings → NULL + EINVAL: "", "z", "rw", "ar", "rwe"

Path handling:
11. Long path (PATH_MAX) accepted
12. Path with spaces accepted
13. Path with embedded null → EINVAL
14. NULL path → EFAULT (or segfault — POSIX is permissive; document choice)

isatty buffering:
15. fopen("/dev/tty", "w") on a tty → buffer mode = _IOLBF
16. fopen("/dev/null", "w") → buffer mode = _IOFBF
17. fopen("/tmp/x", "w") on a regular file → _IOFBF

errno preservation:
18. Failed fopen sets errno appropriately (ENOENT, EACCES, EISDIR)
19. Successful fopen does NOT touch errno

Fd lifecycle:
20. Returned NativeFile fd is a fresh fd (not 0/1/2)
21. fclose closes the fd (verify with fstat returning EBADF after)
22. fclose called twice → second call fails with EINVAL (or detects double-close — POMDP healing in hardened mode)

fdopen:
23. fdopen of a fresh socket fd → NativeFile that supports read/write
24. fdopen of -1 → NULL + EBADF
25. fdopen with mode mismatching the fd open flags → EINVAL

EINTR:
26. fopen on a slow filesystem (LD_PRELOAD a slow openat) returning EINTR → retried transparently

## Done

- All 26 tests in tests/integration/fopen_fdopen_test.rs
- Each runs under both strict and hardened modes
- Tests use the real .so via LD_PRELOAD","notes":"Comprehensive fopen/fdopen tests exist in stdio_abi_test.rs. Unit tests requiring NativeFile streams are marked as needing LD_PRELOAD integration. 98 tests pass, 25 appropriately ignored for vtable validation constraints.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:40:26.393957331Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-13T19:03:03.390402718Z","closed_at":"2026-04-13T19:03:03.389785779Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["constructor","stdio","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.47","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:40:26.393957331Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-9chy.5","title":"stdio: rewrite fopen/fopen64/fdopen to be native-only, returning *mut NativeFile","description":"## Background

The audit pinpoints these as the only unconditional host delegators in stdio_abi.rs. fopen at L676-729 calls host_fopen() under prefer_host_stdio_streams() and otherwise falls back to native openat + registry insert. fdopen at L4060-4108 same pattern. Every other stdio function downstream "checks registry first; delegates to host if alien" — meaning the *only* way alien pointers enter the system is through these two constructors. Eliminate the alien-pointer source and the rest of the cleanup follows naturally.

## Depends on

bd-9chy.1 (NativeFile must be _IO_FILE_plus-shaped before fopen/fdopen can return it without breaking existing pointer-arith assumptions).

## What to do

1. In crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdio_abi.rs, rewrite fopen() (L676-729):
   - Parse mode string into open flags (existing helper).
   - Call native openat raw syscall (already at L703-709 in the fallback branch — promote to primary).
   - Allocate a NativeFile from the slab allocator (or via the registry constructor — verify which exists).
   - Initialize: fd, vtable=&NATIVE_IO_JUMP_T, generation=fresh, mbstate=initial, orientation=Undecided, lock_count=0, open_flags=oflags, fingerprint=fresh siphash.
   - Set buffering mode based on isatty() raw syscall (NOT libc::isatty): _IOLBF if isatty, _IOFBF otherwise.
   - Insert into stream registry.
   - Return as *mut FILE (cast). NEVER call host_fopen.
2. fopen64 is an alias to fopen on 64-bit platforms — verify it stays that way and routes to the native path.
3. fdopen at L4060-4108 same surgery: take the input fd, build a NativeFile shell, set buffering mode based on isatty(fd), insert into registry, return.
4. Remove the prefer_host_stdio_streams() check from both functions. The function itself stays for now (deleted in a separate cleanup bead) but these two callers must not consult it.
5. Confirm the existing native-fallback branch in fopen (L694-729) becomes the primary and only branch.

## Cross-cutting

- The slab allocator for NativeFile should be a fixed-size pool to keep alloc/free deterministic. Coordinate with the malloc crate maintainer.
- isatty must be implemented as a raw ioctl(TCGETS) or fcntl probe — using libc::isatty would re-introduce host delegation.

## Done

- fopen/fopen64/fdopen do not call host_*_fn() under any branch
- A unit test calls fopen("/dev/null", "w"), inspects the returned pointer, confirms its in the registry, and verifies the vtable pointer is set
- Existing fopen tests still pass
- The fopen body has zero references to prefer_host_stdio_streams or host_fopen_fn","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:27:26.044561856Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-13T17:42:20.571271340Z","closed_at":"2026-04-13T17:42:20.570983165Z","close_reason":"Rewrote fopen/fopen64/fdopen to use native NativeFile path. No host delegation. Returns real *mut NativeFile pointers. Uses raw syscalls for isatty check. Registers in native_stream_registry.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["constructor","stdio"]}
 {"id":"bd-9chy.6","title":"stdio: implement popen/pclose natively via posix_spawn + native fdopen","description":"## Background

popen is FILE-acquisition channel #3 in plan v4 §1.10. Today it lives in process_abi.rs or stdio_abi.rs (verify) and almost certainly delegates to host libc since host pipe+fork+fdopen is non-trivial to replicate. After the fopen/fdopen bead lands (bd-9chy.5), popen must build a native NativeFile around the pipe end.

## Depends on

bd-9chy.1 (layout), bd-9chy.5 (native fdopen exists)

## What to do

1. Locate the current popen implementation. Likely in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/process_abi.rs.
2. Replace with: native pipe2 raw syscall, native posix_spawn raw syscall (or fork+execve fallback for kernels without posix_spawn), close the unused pipe end in the parent, pass mode "r" or "w" to native fdopen on the surviving end.
3. pclose: read fd from the NativeFile, close it via native fclose path, then waitpid on the recorded child pid, return the child status. Store the child pid in a NativeFile extension field (add a popen_child_pid: Option<i32> to NativeFileState).
4. Both "r" and "w" modes must work; "re" (close-on-exec) must be honored.
5. An integration test runs `popen("echo hello", "r")`, fgets a line, pclose, asserts return == 0 and the line == "hello\n".

## Edge cases

- SIGCHLD handling: do not install a default SIG_IGN that would orphan zombies; let the user decide.
- popen inside a multi-threaded program: pthread_atfork handlers (separate bead bd-9chy-22 in the locking tier) must run before fork.
- Failure modes: spawn failure must close both pipe ends and return NULL with errno set.

## Done

- popen/pclose are native; no host delegation
- Both modes work; "re" honored
- Integration test passes
- popen_child_pid field added to NativeFileState","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:27:38.977289670Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-13T18:50:03.705447591Z","closed_at":"2026-04-13T18:50:03.704781310Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["constructor","stdio"]}

Acceptance criteria:

 {"id":"bd-reax","title":"EPIC: Replace critical stubs with real implementations or host delegation","description":"The comprehensive audit identified several categories of stub/placeholder implementations:

1. SAFE NO-OP STUBS (~50 functions): Return 0/-1/NULL without doing real work. These are intentional for features we don't support (profiling, NSS, netgroups). LOW priority — they don't crash, just silently no-op.

2. HOST CALL-THROUGH DEBT (~70 symbols): Functions that delegate to host glibc via RTLD_NEXT. These work correctly but represent symbols we haven't implemented natively yet. LOW priority — they work fine via delegation.

3. CORE TERMIOS STUBS: tcgetattr/tcsetattr/tcflush/tcflow in frankenlibc-core return -1. NOT a real issue because the ABI layer (termios_abi.rs) uses raw ioctl syscalls directly and never calls these core stubs.

4. RPC SUBSYSTEM (~68 stubs): Return safe defaults. MEDIUM priority — RPC is legacy but some programs still use it.

5. ELF LOADER INCOMPLETE: Only Phase-1 relocations; IFUNC deferred. MEDIUM priority — affects shared library loading.

RATIONALE: Focus on items that break real programs first. The setjmp/longjmp stubs were already fixed (now delegate to host). The on_exit handler gap was already fixed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"PlumCove","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:55.983549992Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T19:47:38.984372338Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T19:47:38.983957473Z","close_reason":"Both children closed: bd-reax.1 (RPC host delegation) and bd-reax.2 (IFUNC relocation). Remaining categories: safe no-op stubs (intentional for unsupported features like profiling/NSS/netgroups), host call-through (working via delegation), and core termios stubs (ABI layer uses raw ioctl, never calls core stubs). No critical stubs remain.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-reax.1","title":"Add host delegation for critical RPC functions (xdr_*, clnt_*)","description":"FILES: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/rpc_abi.rs
CURRENT: ~68 RPC symbols use rpc_native! macro returning safe defaults (0/NULL).
PROBLEM: Programs using Sun RPC (portmap, NFS tools, ypbind) get silent failures.
FIX: For the most commonly used RPC functions (clntudp_create, clnttcp_create, xdr_int, xdr_string, etc.), resolve the host glibc symbol via our ELF resolver and delegate. Keep the safe-default fallback for symbols not found in host.
CONSIDERATION: RPC is deprecated and being replaced by gRPC. Many modern systems don't even have RPC tools installed. This is low priority unless a specific program needs it.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:56.142778940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T19:17:39.021445416Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T19:17:39.020480090Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-reax.1","depends_on_id":"bd-reax","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:56.142778940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-reax.2","title":"Add IFUNC relocation support to ELF loader","description":"FILES: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/elf/loader.rs:222
CURRENT: Only Phase-1 relocations supported. IFUNC (STT_GNU_IFUNC) relocations are deferred.
PROBLEM: glibc itself uses IFUNC for CPU-specific optimized versions of memcpy, memset, etc. When we try to resolve these from the host ELF, we may get the IFUNC resolver instead of the final address.
FIX: Detect IFUNC symbols and call the resolver function to get the actual implementation address.
IMPACT: Affects host_resolve.rs which parses the glibc ELF to find symbols like malloc, dlvsym, etc. If any of these are IFUNCs, we'd get the wrong address.
CONSIDERATION: On modern x86_64 glibc, malloc and dlvsym are NOT IFUNCs (they're direct symbols), but memcpy/memset ARE IFUNCs. Since we only resolve non-IFUNC symbols from host_resolve.rs, this may not be an immediate problem.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:57.285183747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T19:15:50.515692509Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T19:15:50.514670096Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-reax.2","depends_on_id":"bd-reax","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:57.285183747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
